issue_title: Travis is testing pyexcel-0.2.1
issue_body:
Before 0.2.1 was cut, Travis tests on master had the following results in `pip freeze`

```
pyexcel-io==0.2.0
pyexcel-ods3==0.2.0
pyexcel-xls==0.2.0
pyexcel-xlsx==0.2.0
```

https://travis-ci.org/pyexcel/pyexcel/jobs/126440816#L311 (87ec8c7)

After 0.2.1 was cut, Travis tests on master had the following results in `pip freeze`

```
pyexcel==0.2.1
pyexcel-io==0.2.0
pyexcel-ods3==0.2.0
pyexcel-text==0.2.0
pyexcel-xls==0.2.0
pyexcel-xlsx==0.2.0
```

https://travis-ci.org/pyexcel/pyexcel/jobs/126588417#L319 (badecbf)

The problem is that b0146294 (Apr 29 2016) added `pyexcel-text>=0.2.0` to `tests/requirements.txt` , and pyexcel-text 0.2.0 depends on pyexcel 2.1.0, so pip installs it from pypi before running the tests.

The very simple solution is for travis to re-install pyexcel[master] before running the tests.

However there is a larger problem of dependency management.

If we change the dependencies of `pyexcel` , pyexcel installed pyexcel-text[master], which
installed pyexcel>=0.2.0 (from pypi), the old dependencies of pyexcel>=0.2.0 (from pypi) are installed, which means we cant test on Travis with reduced/alternative dependencies, unless we forcibly uninstall them after `rnd_requirements.txt` and `tests/requirements.txt`.

An example of this is #38, where I want to downgrade texttable on Python 2.  pyexcel>=0.2.0 (from pypi) will install texttable 0.8.2, whereas I want to install texttable>=0.8.1 .  When I install texttable 0.8.1 using apt, pip upgrades it to texttable 0.8.2, so it wont be tested.

Another related problem is that master is not incompatible with pyexcel-io 0.1.0, yet the requirements still show pyexcel-io>=0.1.0 .

And there is one solution for all of those.
Firstly, to install `pyexcel-text[master]`, we need to also install `.` at the same time.  However bugs in pip/setuptools means that alone doesnt work.  First we need to install everything in `rnd_requirements.txt` *without dependencies*.  That installs the master of `-text`, `-io`, etc.
Then reprocess it with dependencies.  Patch coming shortly...

issue_title: Rename and refactor CallSettings
issue_body:
Currently we have CallSettings and CallOptions, and it's hard to say their roles and the differences.

Actually CallSettings is the settings (or configurations) for an API call (or a method), such as the page descriptors or bundling descriptors, retry parameters.

On the other hand, CallOptions is the optional data to modify the behavior of individual invocation of the methods.

To me,
- CallSettings isn't a very clear name. 'Call-' prefix sounds like per-call (per-invocation) thing. Maybe, `MethodSettings`?
- the 'merge' method to create a new call settings doesn't look a good design after the redesign of create_api_call.  I feel like CallSettings (or MethodSettings) should hold the default CallOptions instance, and CallOptions instance should have the `merge()` method to create the actual options for the invocation.

Thoughts?

issue_title: Runtime failure on architectures without long-double FFTW
issue_body: I have updated the [Debian packaging](https://buildd.debian.org/status/package.php?p=pyfftw) with release `0.10.4` and the build now fails for some architectures, some of these considered major. On `armel`, `armhf` and `mips[el]`, it seems that importing the module triggers an import of the long-double FFTW although the latter is not available and not being built against.

issue_title: prepare_transfer method
issue_body:
Currently it is the following:
```
In [14]: cid = 0

In [15]: condition = creation_tx['transaction']['conditions'][cid]

In [16]: transfer_input = {
   ....:     'fulfillment': condition['condition']['details'],
   ....:     'input': {
   ....:          'cid': cid,
   ....:          'txid': creation_tx['id'],
   ....:      },
   ....:      'owners_before': condition['owners_after'],
   ....: }
   ....: 

In [17]: prepared_transfer_tx = bdb.transactions.prepare(
   ....:     operation='TRANSFER',
   ....:     asset=creation_tx['transaction']['asset'],
   ....:     inputs=transfer_input,
   ....:     owners_after=bob.verifying_key,
   ....: )
   ....: 
```
while it could be simply:
prepared_transfer_tx = bdb.transactions.prepare_transfer(input_tx, output_id, owners_after, metadata) 
Notice that the operation, transfer_input, asset are all derived from the input_tx (full transaction, not only the tx_id). The complex transfer_input object is legacy of the common code, but can easily be abstracted away in the driver until the common code is fixed

issue_body:
```
No installers seem to be present and working on your system; fix that or try running certbot with the "certonly" command
```

This is one of the most common messages, received when running certbot on an Nginx installation, but it is confusing to a new user. What's an installer? Is it my mistake or certbot's that there are no installers? What does "certonly" do?

Instead how about:

```
Certbot doesn't know how to automatically configure the web server on this system. However, it can still get a certificate for you. Please run "certbot[-auto] certonly" to do so. You'll need to manually configure your web server to use the resulting certificate.
```

issue_title: Add support for using an API key for Google API calls
issue_body:
Google APIs support passing an API key for access, billing and quota purposes; this is *usually* completely subsumed by providing an OAuth2 token, but not always.

This is used in several places, eg [GCS access with no token](https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/json_api/v1/how-tos/authorizing#APIKey) or various [ML APIs](https://cloud.google.com/vision/docs/auth#using_an_api_key). This is also used for access to new or limited access APIs, as well as for some internal auth scenarios.

Currently, Datalab doesn't have any support for this. Adding it would involve:
1. adding a new global var (probably to the `Context` object)
1. optionally allowing an additional header (`X-Google-Project-Override: apikey`) for some internal scenarios.
1. including this as the `key` query param when constructing a URL.

2. The setup.py should be improved. If the version of liblz4 found is lower than requirements, bundled LZ4 library should be used.

issue_title: Option shared for remote execution of commands with shared or partially shared file systems.
issue_body:
The remote execution feature is general enough to cover cases when remote host shares or partially shares local file system, but we should document these cases and make our documentation more general.

In the shared case, No file copying is needed. There is no need to specify `to_host`, `from_host`, `path_map` and `map_vars`  (if we decide to keep it). The command will be invoked in the remote machine. 

SoS will in its current implementation still tries to `rsync` job file to the remote server. This might work just fine but we could potentially tell sos this is not needed. Note that missing `path_map` is not enough to tell SoS about shared file system because remote and local files systems might have the same names.

In the partially shared case (e.g. a shared data volume to both local and remote host), users will need `on_host`, but with less or no `to_host`, `from_host`, and `path_map`.

I would propose that we add a key `shared` to `CONFIG['hosts'][host]` that specifies what directories are shared between local and remote machines. For example, if `shared='/'`, we know everything is shared so no file will be copied.

issue_title: Remote execution of workflow
issue_body:
#475 

It was proposed that there can be a global remote mode where all input, output, and depends are supposed to be on remote host. This sounds like an appealing method to execute an entire workflow remotely, but we will need to define the behavior of SoS clearly before we even think of an interface.

Questions:

1. What about files that are not input, output, or depends?

2. What if the logic of a next step depends on the result file that is currently on the remote machine?

3. What to do with simple operations such as `len(input)` when `input: '*.txt'`? I suppose the current `input: `remote('*.txt')` has the same problem.

4. Would it be easier just to execute entire workflow on the remote server?

issue_title: STL dataset returns test fold if fold is misspecified
issue_body:
If you miss-spell, or otherwise pass an invalid fold to the STL dataset it will set the data and labels to that of the test set.

Can be seen here:

https://github.com/pytorch/vision/blob/fa377c47d0f444f9497678b6641b360cb665f8f9/torchvision/datasets/stl10.py#L75

Other datasets appear to raise a `ValueError` in their constructor when passed an invalid fold:
https://github.com/pytorch/vision/blob/fa377c47d0f444f9497678b6641b360cb665f8f9/torchvision/datasets/mnist.py#L202

Would be good to do so for STL-10 also.

issue_title: Plasma managers die when running stressful workloads on a large cluster.
issue_body:
I've seen the following problem arise for stressful workloads on 200 nodes (m4.16xlarge).

From the logs, some plasma managers and some local schedulers kill themselves because they go long enough without sending a heartbeat. In the past this arose because a plasma manager could be blocked in a call to `connect` when trying to establish a TCP connection to ship an object to another plasma manager. When too many managers did this at once, some would retry and due to exponential backoff some would end up blocked for tens of seconds. I think the relevant line is the one below.

https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/6601bb5f9e73449667192926229ac4c6b1e083f9/src/plasma/plasma_manager.cc#L822

This was addressed in #661.

This time, it looks like the blocking call that is taking too long is the following.

https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/6601bb5f9e73449667192926229ac4c6b1e083f9/src/common/state/redis.cc#L612-L613

Now, each individual synchronous Redis command there isn't taking too long (the slowest ones according to my print statements took 300 or 400 milliseconds), but we're doing it in a loop at

https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/6601bb5f9e73449667192926229ac4c6b1e083f9/src/common/state/redis.cc#L710-L713

This is inside of `object_table_redis_subscribe_to_notifications_callback`, so if one manager is trying to fetch a particular object, and that object is present on 200 other nodes, and if the primary Redis shard happens to be somewhat overwhelmed (e.g., it is exporting tons of remote function definitions to tons and tons of workers), then that loop can take tens of seconds and kill the plasma manager.

issue_title: Using websockify with NAT for multiple VNC servers across different PCs
issue_body:
Hello,
I have a question regarding usage of websockify with NAT: say that I need to have a publicly available HTTPS server which sits in front of the NAT clients. In this scenario, one NAT client is actually PC with running VNC server and there can be many clients like this. Lets say that adding NAT client(s) does not have to be 'dynamic' (on the fly) - it is desirable, but not mandatory. 

Is it possible (and if yes - how?) that users from the outside world, sees only HTTPS server (some login page) and depending on selected NAT client (VNC server) be connected to it. I manage to do this using a port from the pool of opened ports in the given range, so external user may connect to VNC behind NAT using websocket. 

My client however is concerned that this is a security breach (due multiple opened ports for websockify to use) and would prefer that we use only one websockify port to achieve this. Plus, that port must not be available from the public network. In short, he would prefer that 443 is used for both HTTPS server (NGINX) and also websockify to target multiple VNC servers behind NAT. Additionally, multiple users can access such system at the same time. I, on the other hand am not convinced that this is possible (at least part considering sharing 443 between HTTPS server and websockify). 

I tried to find a solution on google, but the closest thing I found is to use HTTPS server that targets multiple VNCs by tampering systemd processes - but even in this solution, different ports on HTTPS server were used for different VNC servers (each target VNC server has dedicated VNC port at HTTPS server PC); and number of possible VNC clients is measured in 100s, so extending number of daemons that runs  websockify is not practical (at least I think so), plus, most of the time users are idle and not using a connection.

Thank you all in advance.

Best regards

issue_title: Assocmodel: extensions datamodel should support disjunctions
issue_body:
Currently the model assumes extensions are a conjunctive list of relational expressions. Annots with disjunctive expressions are translated to multiple annots. This is semantically equivalent but problematic as number of annots change.

The model will change to support disjunctions

Hi a small feature request, instead of downloading all the bands by default i.e, when bands are not explicitly passed as an argument. Download only Metafiles.

Example, for below code, download only the metafiles without including all the bands :

`metafiles = ['tileInfo', 'preview']

request = AwsTileRequest(tile=tile_name, time=time, aws_index=aws_index,
                         metafiles=metafiles, data_folder=data_folder,
                         data_source=DataSource.SENTINEL2_L1C)

request.save_data()`

issue_title: Make encoding behavior of simplejson possible to override
issue_body:
I would really like to be able to control how a variable is encoded directly.  Currently, if a variable is of a type recognized by simplejson it will be encoded automatically, with no imput on my part.  I've bumped up against the inability to override atomic encode actions repeatedly, and it has made using this library terribly unpleasant.

Specifically...

I wanted to override encoding so that certain strings would be encoded without surrounding quotes, for code generation.  The way the code there is no way to do this short of hacking the module, which is silly.

I want to serialize a graph with cycles.  It is really easy to get rid of cycles by ignoring edges to already visited nodes in the graph when moving outward from a visited node.  I would love to add this bit of logic, but I can't do that without totally rewriting your class (ironically you already do this with markers, but since I can't get into the code anywhere, that is pretty useless to me).

Ideally, an api for this would let you specify a "translator" function, as well as input/output filters.
